  • All Sources
  • AI Usage Log

References

References

Bivens, Josh, Hilary Wething, and Ben Zipperer. 2026. “Rising Inequality Is the Root of Affordability Problems.” Economic Policy Institute. https://www.epi.org/blog/rising-inequality-is-the-root-of-affordability-problems.
Cafer, Anne, Gina Mann, Shrinidhi Ramachandran, and Mark Kaiser. 2018. “National Food Affordability: A County-Level Analysis.” Preventing Chronic Disease 15 (September): E115. https://doi.org/10.5888/pcd15.180079.
Center on Budget and Policy Priorities. 2024. “The Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP).” https://www.cbpp.org/research/food-assistance/the-supplemental-nutrition-assistance-program-snap.
Federal Reserve Bank of New York. 2024. “What Was up with Grocery Prices?” Liberty Street Economics. https://libertystreeteconomics.newyorkfed.org/2024/07/what-was-up-with-grocery-prices.
Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis. 2025. “Federal Reserve Economic Data (FRED).” https://fred.stlouisfed.org.
Food Research & Action Center. 2021. “Thrifty Food Plan Adjustment.” https://frac.org/wp-content/uploads/Thrifty-Food-Plan-Adjustment-One-Pager.pdf.
Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health. 2025. “What Is SNAP and Why Does It Matter?” https://publichealth.jhu.edu/2025/what-is-snap-and-why-does-it-matter.
National Council on Aging. 2024. “How Much Have Food Prices Gone up and How Can I Save?” https://www.ncoa.org/article/how-much-have-food-prices-gone-up-and-how-can-i-save.
Rural Advancement Foundation International USA. 2024. “Grocery Gap Atlas.” https://grocerygapatlas.rafiusa.org/map.
Saksena, Monica J., Abigail M. Okrent, Tobenna D. Anekwe, Clare Cho, Christopher Dicken, Anne Effland, Howard Elitzak, et al. 2020. “Average Share of Income Spent on Food in the United States Remained Relatively Steady from 2000 to 2019.” Amber Waves, November. https://www.ers.usda.gov/amber-waves/2020/november/average-share-of-income-spent-on-food-in-the-united-states-remained-relatively-steady-from-2000-to-2019.
Stewart, Hayden. 2022. “U.S. Fruit and Vegetable Affordability on the Thrifty Food Plan Depends on Purchasing Power and Safety Net Supports.” International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health 19 (5): 2772. https://doi.org/10.3390/ijerph19052772.
University of British Columbia Food Services. 2023. “9 Tips to Save Money on Groceries.” https://food.ubc.ca/9-tips-to-save-money-on-groceries.
U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics. 2024. “Consumer Expenditures—Annual News Release.” https://www.bls.gov/news.release/cesan.nr0.htm.
U.S. Census Bureau. 2023. “County Business Patterns: 2023.” https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/cbp.html.
———. 2024. “Small Area Income and Poverty Estimates (SAIPE) Program.” https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/saipe.html.
U.S. Department of Agriculture, Economic Research Service. 2024. “Retail Trends.” https://www.ers.usda.gov/topics/food-markets-prices/retailing-wholesaling/retail-trends.
———. 2025a. “Food Expenditure Series.” https://www.ers.usda.gov/data-products/food-expenditure-series.
———. 2025b. “Food Security in the U.S.: Key Statistics and Graphics.” https://www.ers.usda.gov/topics/food-nutrition-assistance/food-security-in-the-us/key-statistics-graphics.
———. 2025c. “Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP): Key Statistics and Research.” https://www.ers.usda.gov/topics/food-nutrition-assistance/supplemental-nutrition-assistance-program-snap/key-statistics-and-research.
U.S. Department of Agriculture, Food and Nutrition Service. 2025a. “SNAP Restaurant Meals Program.” https://www.fns.usda.gov/snap/retailer/restaurant-meals-program.
———. 2025b. “Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) Participation and Costs.” https://www.fns.usda.gov/pd/supplemental-nutrition-assistance-program-snap.
———. 2025c. “USDA Food Plans.” https://www.fns.usda.gov/research/cnpp/usda-food-plans.
———. 2025d. “USDA Food Plans: Cost of Food Monthly Reports.” https://www.fns.usda.gov/research/cnpp/usda-food-plans/cost-food-monthly-reports.
USA.gov. 2025. “Food Help.” https://www.usa.gov/food-help.
